summary: Systems architecture review accepts the M5 Opportunity Detail and limited status-update implementation. The dynamic detail page is server-rendered and reads one persisted Opportunity through lib/opportunities.ts, the status write path is a thin local PATCH boundary that permits only saved/discarded/build_next, view models remain plain serializable objects, missing resources use safe not-found behavior, and the working tree does not introduce MVP Spec, schema, package, scoring, scan orchestration, auth/session, deployment, or broad API creep.

notes: Product changes are confined to allowed paths: app/opportunities/[id]/**, app/api/opportunities/[id]/**, components/**, lib/opportunities.ts, tests/**, and README.md.
The implementation handoff under project/messages/outbox is JustGoal coordination state requested by the task workflow, not product implementation creep.

No working tree changes were found in prisma/**, package.json, package-lock.json, lib/scoring.ts, lib/db.ts, lib/env.ts, lib/schemas.ts, services/**, agents/**, app/api/scans/**, app/api/radar-tasks/**, app/page.tsx, app/layout.tsx, app/globals.css, docs/**, or mock-data/**.

evidence: app/opportunities/[id]/page.tsx:24 calls getOpportunityDetailData(id) from the shared helper instead of importing Prisma or db in the route.
app/opportunities/[id]/page.tsx:27 and app/opportunities/[id]/page.tsx:34 call notFound() for helper-level not-found errors and null detail results.
app/api/opportunities/[id]/route.ts:3 exports only PATCH through createOpportunityStatusPatchHandler(), keeping the route file thin.
lib/opportunities.ts:319 centralizes the persisted detail read with db.opportunity.findUnique and the existing opportunity include.
lib/opportunities.ts:371 centralizes the status update and selects only id, status, and updatedAt from Prisma after writing.
Search for Prisma/db imports found them confined to lib/opportunities.ts; app/opportunities, app/api/opportunities, and components do not import @prisma/client or @/lib/db.
lib/opportunities.ts:42 defines OPPORTUNITY_STATUS_UPDATE_STATUSES as saved, discarded, and build_next only, separate from the broader persisted status enum at lib/opportunities.ts:35.
lib/opportunities.ts:593 parses update input through that narrow allow-list and rejects new, built, non-strings, and arbitrary values with OpportunityStatusValidationError.
app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts:74 parses only body.status, and app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts:123 calls the injected updateStatus(opportunityId, status) boundary with no extra body fields.
lib/opportunities.ts:380 updates only data: { status } on the Opportunity row.
lib/opportunities.ts:496 builds OpportunityDetailViewModel by extending the existing serialized card model, preserving ISO date strings from serializeOpportunity and adding detail-only plain fields.

lib/opportunities.ts:500 normalizes persisted scoreExplanation into a string map, lib/opportunities.ts:516 builds scoreBreakdownItems, and lib/opportunities.ts:525 normalizes persisted killCriteria into a string array.
lib/opportunities.ts:956 derives toolConcept from rawAnalysis.opportunityAnalysis.toolConcept without exposing rawAnalysis to components.
tests/opportunities.test.ts:145 asserts the detail view model includes persisted score/analysis context and tests/opportunities.test.ts:179 asserts JSON.stringify(detail) succeeds.
app/opportunities/[id]/page.tsx:27 and app/opportunities/[id]/page.tsx:34 route missing detail data through framework notFound().
app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts:50 returns a shared INVALID_JSON error for malformed JSON.

app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts:82 maps invalid status requests to sanitized 400 VALIDATION_ERROR responses and app/api/opportunities/[id]/_api.ts:96 maps missing opportunities to sanitized 404 NOT_FOUND responses.
tests/opportunities.test.ts:369 covers malformed JSON and tests/opportunities.test.ts:394 covers missing id/missing opportunity without leaking a secret id.

components/OpportunityDetail.tsx:14 receives an OpportunityDetailViewModel prop and renders title/keyword, score, breakdown/explanations, SERP/toolability, monetization, risk/build, kill criteria, status controls, and run context without importing Prisma or duplicating queries.

components/OpportunityStatusControls.tsx:21 exposes only Save, Discard, and Build next actions, and components/OpportunityStatusControls.tsx:60 sends only { status: nextStatus } to the local PATCH endpoint.
components/OpportunityStatusControls.tsx:113 marks the active action with aria-pressed and disables the active/pending buttons.

README.md:313 documents the Opportunity Detail and Status Updates slice, README.md:331 documents the local single-admin status boundary, and README.md:342 lists non-goals including no MVP Spec, schema migration, package change, scan orchestration change, scoring formula change, auth/session, deployment, or GitHub Actions.
Repository status/diff inspection showed no package, Prisma schema, lib/scoring.ts, services, agents, scans API, radar-tasks API, app shell, global CSS, docs, or mock-data edits.

tests_run.result: PASS: working tree shows README.md, lib/opportunities.ts, tests/opportunities.test.ts modified; new app/api/opportunities/[id], app/opportunities/[id], OpportunityDetail/OpportunityStatusControls components, and implementation handoff present.
PASS: confirmed the product file set is within the task's allowed paths, with only the implementation handoff under project/messages/outbox as workflow coordination state.
PASS: 14 focused opportunity tests passed across 3 suites.
PASS: no whitespace errors; Git printed LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings for README.md, lib/opportunities.ts, and tests/opportunities.test.ts only.
PASS per main-thread evidence supplied with this review request: 60 tests passed.
PASS per main-thread evidence supplied with this review request: Next.js production build exited 0 with nonfatal local Windows SWC optional-binary warnings.

decisions: ACCEPT: The dynamic detail route is server-rendered and uses the shared opportunity helper for data access.
ACCEPT: Prisma read/write ownership remains in lib/opportunities.ts; UI components and route files remain thin and serializable.
ACCEPT: The mutation boundary is intentionally narrow: only saved, discarded, and build_next can be requested, and only Opportunity.status is persisted.
ACCEPT: The API error surface is safe for invalid JSON, invalid statuses, missing ids, missing opportunities, and unexpected failures.
ACCEPT: The implementation shows stored score/risk/monetization/SERP/tool context without recalculating scoring, invoking agents, or adding MVP Spec behavior.
ACCEPT: The working tree stays inside product allowed paths and avoids the forbidden schema/package/scoring/API/provider/auth/deployment creep listed in the task packet.
risks: The PATCH endpoint remains unauthenticated by design for the local single-admin MVP; public deployment still requires a later auth/session/security slice.
Status changes are simple field updates with no discard reason, audit log, multi-user conflict handling, or cross-tab refresh; this matches scope but should not be mistaken for a workflow system.
Tool concept rendering depends on the persisted rawAnalysis.opportunityAnalysis.toolConcept shape from newer scan outputs; older rows without that JSON degrade to available toolability/score text.

README still contains the prior Dashboard/List slice's historical non-goal line that says that earlier slice did not implement detail/status routes; the new Opportunity Detail section immediately below documents the current slice clearly, so this is not a systems blocker.
requested_action: Proceed to domain_designer, security_reviewer, and qa_reviewer review gates. Systems architecture accepts with no requested product-code changes.
